svg.text: The Architecture of Intent — On One Page
Three questions, five activities, one canvas. Every load-bearing list in the framework, in the place it does work.

g.text: What is this system trying to achieve?
The goal that licenses the delegation.
What it must never do, who it can't act for, what state it can't touch.
How will we know it's working?
The signals that confirm or contradict each call.

svg.text: Irreversible · regulated · safety-critical → elevate one step toward Orchestrator
Set independently within the archetype's envelope. Not a single "automation level."

g.text: What can be undone, at what cost

svg.text: CANONICAL SPEC TEMPLATE — 12 sections, colored by dimension

g.text: Frames the goal that licenses delegation
What the system must do, not how
What the agent may reach, with what scope

svg.text: BIND PATTERNS — pull by what the spec implies (~50 patterns in 8 categories)

g.text: System prompt · skill file · tool manifest ·
data · blast radius · rate limit · degrade

svg.text: PICK OVERSIGHT — proportional to autonomy × reversibility
DIAGNOSE FAILURES — 7 categories partitioned by FIX LOCUS, not by symptom
STRUCTURAL AMENDMENTS — production findings → spec / manifest / CI / framework version (never only the prompt)
FRAME · SPECIFY · DELEGATE · VALIDATE · EVOLVE — the five activities of the Architecture of Intent. The dashed loop is EVOLVE in motion: each diagnosed failure becomes a structural amendment.
